title: tylophora parviflora
intro: Tylophora parviflora, also known as 'small-flowered Tylophora', is a perennial climber plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and anti-histamine effects, helping to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Tylophora parviflora include its ability to act as an immunomodulator, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Tylophora parviflora are often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or powdered extracts, and are typically consumed after consulting with a healthcare professional.
